_doc: VIVO org-unit redirect crosswalk (runbook D-10..D-13). VIVO /display/org-u<N> 301 -> Scholars /departments/<slug> or /centers/<slug>. Divisions roll up to their parent department.
_why_handwritten: VIVO org-u<N> ids are VIVO-internal sequential ids and do NOT match Scholars org codes (departments use WCM N-codes like N1280; centers use slug-codes). There is no automatic key in the Scholars DB, so this crosswalk is hand-built and reviewed.
_status: All 16 trafficked org ids resolved 2026-05-30 (all are departments). vivoName read off VIVO via a single gentle sequential range-GET sweep; scholarsTarget matched to the Scholars department slug. Re-run the procedure below for any NEW org id that shows up in future traffic.
_how_to_resolve: 1. The vivoOrgId list below is the set of org units with real traffic, extracted from docs/data/vivo-pageview-baseline/vivo-top-pages-2026-05-30.csv (NOT a full crawl).
2. For each entry, read its name off VIVO: GET https://vivo.weill.cornell.edu/display/org-<vivoOrgId> and take the <title> (the org name). Range-request the first ~16KB so you only pull the <head>.
3. WARNING: VIVO crashes under aggressive querying. Resolve SEQUENTIALLY, THROTTLED, lowest-id-first, one request at a time. Do this BEFORE cutover while VIVO is healthy. Never parallel/bulk fetch.
4. Match vivoName to the Scholars department/center and set scholarsTarget to the site-relative path. Divisions -> their parent department's /departments/<slug>.
5. Leave scholarsTarget null for any org with no Scholars equivalent (merged/defunct) -> it falls through to 404 (D-13).
6. Re-run `npm run etl:vivo-redirect`; non-null entries compile into output/vivo-org-redirect.map.
